costs: Legislative capital required to amend primary legislation against an organized directors' lobby with direct access to top leadership (F-live-political_feasibility-07, F-live-legal_and_governance-01)
Teacher redeployment and retraining costs amid existing 16,000-teacher shortfall and workforce aging (F-live-implementation_planning-07)
Risk of transition-year capacity shortfalls analogous to Poland's 2017 double-cohort crisis if phasing is mismanaged (F-live-polish_reform-05)
content.equity_impact: Highest potential structural equity impact among all proposals by directly targeting the mechanism most strongly and consistently linked in the evidence to Hungary's outlier between-school variance and SES-performance gradient; however, does not by itself resolve Roma segregation (a distinct legal-systemic issue) or primary-school choice sorting unless explicitly bundled
expected_benefits: Cross-national evidence associates comprehensive delayed tracking bundled with curriculum reform with reduced inequality of opportunity (F-live-finnish_reform-05, F-live-portuguese_reform-05)
Removes the specific age-10/12 selection gate identified as producing Hungary's outlier between-school variance (F-live-international_comparison-02, F-live-legal_and_governance-06)
Long-run Finnish evidence shows positive though modest attainment and earnings effects from comprehensive delay (F-live-international_comparison-08)
content.goal: Eliminate age-10/12 academic tracking by converting the 6/8-year entry points into a single comprehensive lower-secondary structure with first selection deferred to age 14 or later

implementation_steps.action: Draft and pass amendment to Act CXC of 2011 phasing out 6/8-year gimnázium entry points over a defined multi-cohort transition (minimum 5-7 years, informed by Polish reversal costs)
Develop unified lower-secondary national curriculum bundling academic content previously differentiated between gimnázium and general tracks
Redeploy or retrain gimnázium teaching staff into unified lower-secondary institutions, accounting for existing 16,000-teacher shortage and aging workforce
Introduce catchment-area and anti-sorting rules for primary/lower-secondary enrollment to prevent free-choice mechanisms from replicating tracking effects informally
Establish independent monitoring baseline given documented disputes over performance and segregation statistics, to track equity effects through transition

mechanisms: Raise statutory tracking age following the Finnish precedent of comprehensive schooling to 16 and Poland's 1999 delay to 16, both associated with reduced inequality when bundled with curriculum reform (F-live-international_comparison-07, F-live-international_comparison-08, F-live-portuguese_reform-05)
Bundle structural delay with catchment/anti-sorting rules addressing the independent segregation mechanism of free primary-school choice (F-live-portuguese_reform-06, F-live-polish_reform-07)

Phase transition over multiple cohort years to avoid Poland's 2017 single-year 8+4 reversal shock (double-cohort crisis, 3,173 unplaced Warsaw students) (F-live-polish_reform-05, F-live-international_comparison-07)

Address the four-selection-point structure (early-childhood, primary choice, age-10/12, post-16) recognizing that tracking-age reform alone has limited systemic effect without addressing other junctures (F-live-legal_and_governance-08)
risks: Poland's 2017 reversal demonstrates that abrupt, non-phased structural change produces severe capacity shortfalls and job-loss disruption; a comparable Hungarian misstep is plausible given similar administrative capacity constraints (F-live-polish_reform-05, F-live-international_comparison-07)
Constitutional free-choice guarantee may be invoked to challenge catchment/anti-sorting rules bundled with the reform (F-live-legal_and_governance-02)

Finland's own recent PISA decline (64-point fall 2003-2022) and emergence of informal 'emphasized classes' sorting shows structural delay alone does not guarantee durable equity gains absent sustained systemic commitment (F-live-finnish_reform-07, F-live-finnish_reform-08, F-live-finnish_reform-09)
Church/foundation/private schools outside Klebelsberg jurisdiction may resist or be exempted, preserving a segregated selective channel outside state control (F-live-legal_and_governance-07)
Gimnázium directors as veto players could mobilize to block or reverse legislation (F-live-political_feasibility-07)
content.title: Structural delay of first tracking to age 14+ with unified comprehensive lower-secondary phase
